steps.description: Substrates were etched with buffered HF solution (pH = 4.5) to prepare the surface.
Substrates were annealed at 950 °C under Argon flow for 2 hours to restore high-quality step terraces.
A small area on the substrate was covered with acetone-soluble photoresist (Megaposit SPR3612) as a lift-off pattern.
An amorphous layer (20-30 nm) of water-soluble Sr3Al2O6 was grown at room temperature by PLD from an in-house prepared Sr3Al2O6 target.
The photoresist was removed using acetone, leaving a clean area on the substrate.

SrIrO3 layer was grown on top of the entire sample by pulsed laser deposition at 700 °C under oxygen pressure of 30 mTorr (some samples at 15 mTorr). Target to substrate distance was 84 mm, laser fluence was 2 J/cm2. Growth was monitored in situ using RHEED.

equipment.name: pulsed laser deposition system (PLD/MBE 2300)
equipment.settings: 700 °C, 30 mTorr O2, target-substrate distance 84 mm, laser fluence 2 J/cm2

steps.description: The sample was immersed in water for several hours and lightly sonicated to dissolve Sr3Al2O6 and remove the SrIrO3 layer grown above it, leaving the SrIrO3 layer in the area previously protected by the photoresist.

equipment.name: pulsed laser deposition system (PLD/MBE 2300)
equipment.settings: 700 °C, 30 mTorr O2, laser fluence 2 J/cm2, target-substrate distance 84 mm

synthesis.notes: Films were grown in a layer-by-layer mode and exhibit high crystalline quality and step terraces at the surface. Substrate size was 10x10 mm2. Some samples were grown under 15 mTorr O2 instead of 30 mTorr. Thicker films were used in EC-AFM experiments conducted in KOH due to higher dissolution rates. The lift-off patterning with Sr3Al2O6 was used to create a sharp step between the film and substrate for AFM thickness quantification.

steps.description: Exposure of epitaxial SrIrO3 thin films to acidic or alkaline electrolytes.
Electrochemical leaching of Sr2+ cations from the SrIrO3 surface during the pre-catalytic region or oxygen evolution reaction (OER).
Formation of a Sr-deficient, disordered surface layer (Sr1-xIrO3-y) through topochemical de-insertion of Sr compensated by Ir oxidation.

synthesis.notes: The Sr1-xIrO3-y layer is a surface degradation product of SrIrO3. In H2SO4, the layer reaches a thickness of approximately 3-4 nm and is highly disordered/amorphous. Sr leaching precedes the total dissolution of the perovskite.
